title: Purely Indulgent Algerian Almond Tagine
description: This vegan-friendly dish features almond milk and is simmered to perfection in an agave leaf vessel, giving it a unique and indulgent flavor, with a grassy and bouncy texture from the nimono-simmer method.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the almond milk, agave nectar, olive oil, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour the mixture into an agave leaf vessel, if available, or a large ceramic tagine.
Cover the vessel with a lid or foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid or foil and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together.
Stir in the sliced almonds and continue baking for 5 minutes, or until they are lightly toasted.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional sliced almonds if desired.
